title: Monday to Salesforce
excerpt:
Has anyone used Monday to Salesforce integration to create a case in Salesforce? 
I’m looking at using a Monday.com form to ingest credit requests, which I then want to use to create a case in SFDC with some of that info…

posts.author: MelB (Melanie Broxis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Has anyone used Monday to Salesforce integration to create a case in Salesforce?
I’m looking at using a Monday.com form to ingest credit requests, which I then want to use to create a case in SFDC with some of that information, which will then follow a approval workflow in salesforce. When approved, it will tell Monday.com to update the status to the next stage.

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
For integrating Monday.com with Salesforce to create and manage cases, we’ll use a Monday.com form to collect credit requests. These form submissions will trigger the creation of a new case in Salesforce with the relevant information. We will then design an approval workflow within Salesforce. Once a case is approved, Salesforce will communicate back to Monday.com to update the status to the next stage, ensuring seamless and automated tracking.

The integration can be managed with tools like Zapier or Make.com, which enable smooth data flow between Monday.com and Salesforce without extensive coding. We’ll set up triggers and actions that handle form submissions, case creation, approval status checks, and status updates.

posts.author: maxwelllord5768 (Maxwell Lord)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve worked on a similar setup before, and the simplest way was using monday.com Salesforce integration through Zapier or Make.com. The flow I used was pretty straightforward:

  1. A form submission on Monday automatically triggered a new case in Salesforce.

  2. Once the case was approved in Salesforce, the update pushed back into Monday so the team could see the status in real-time.

  3. I made sure to map all fields carefully so data stayed consistent between both platforms.

  4. Testing everything first in a sandbox saved me a lot of rework later.

This approach kept things simple without heavy custom development. If you still can’t get the exact workflow you’re after, it’s always a good idea to reach out to Salesforce Monday integration partners like DGT27, who can fine-tune the setup for your specific use case.
